rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RADIANCE OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Petition To Authorize Approval Of Amended And Restated Declaration Of Covenants, Conditions And Restrictions Of Radiance At Mission Bay And Amended And Restated Bylaws Of Radiance Owners Association
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2022 line 1. HEARING ON PETITION TO AUTHORIZE APPROVAL OF AMENDED AND RESTATED DECLARATION OF COVENANTS, CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS OF RADIANCE AT MISSION BAY AND AMENDED AND RESTATED BYLAWS OF RADIANCE OWNERS ASSOCIATION is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO APARTMENT ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2022 line 2. HEARING FOR WRIT OF MANDATE Hearing Required to address: (1) Do the petitioners seek that the entire Ordinance No.18-22 is invalid, void and unenforceable or only (prayer for relief 2) or only the non-payment of rent part; (2) Can the City eliminate non-payment of rent as a ground for eviction; (3) Can the City require a 30-day written warning? 60-day? 365-day?; (4) is a warning notice a procedural step preceding/or a substantive limitation on landlord's ability to proceed under CCP 1161(2), i.e. "a tenant is guilty of unlawful detainer when he or she continues in possession.. after default in the payment of rent and three days' notice, excluding Saturdays and Sundays and other judicial holidays, in writing, requiring its payment have been served upon him or her." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE VS. 166 GEARY STREET RETAIL OWNER LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For: (1) Appointment Of Receiver, And; (2) Preliminary Injunction In Aid Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR: 1) APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER, AND; 2) PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IN AID OF RECEIVER is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service for the motion on file as of May 26, 2022. The POS is not executed by a person purported to have accomplished the service ("causing the documents to be placed in a sealed FEDEX envelope and the envelope to be delivered to Fedex" and "I am readily familiar with the firm's practice of collection and processing correspondence for mailing. Under that practice it would be deposited with the U.S. Postal Service on that same day with postage thereon fully prepaid in the ordinary course of business.") and contrary to the declaration that service was accomplished by causing transmission by electronic mail to the "email address set forth below" there is no e-mail address set forth. The Court further notes that there is no proof of service for the summons and complaint on file as of May 26, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: XIAOQI WU VS. GINA FINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Date For Demurrer Of Defendant Gina Finn To Complaint Of Plaintiff Xiaowi Wu
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT'S RE-NOTICE OF HEARING DATE FOR DEMURRER OF DEFENDANT GINA FINN TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF XIAOWI WU is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice of Demurrer is to the entire Complaint, while points and authorities only address the third cause of action. Demurrer to the entire complaint is not supported and therefore is overruled as complaint states cause of action for forcible entry and detainer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: XIAOQI WU VS. GINA FINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Re-Notice Of Hearing Date For Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint Of Plaintiff Xiaoqi Wu
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 27, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT KOURTNEY MCEACHIN'S RE-NOTICE OF HEARING DATE FOR MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF XIAOQI WU is DENIED. Authorities cited by the moving party address contract/rental value. Damages sought in this action are hold-over tort damages. No authority is cited for the proposition that these types of damages are subject to the same considerations as discussed in the cited cases. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
